# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";

const middleware = async (req: NextRequest) => {
    const token = req.cookies["auth_token"]; // Get the auth token from cookies
  
    if (!token) {
        return new Response('Unauthorized', { status: 401 }); // If no token, respond with Unauthorized
    } else {
      try {
          const decodedToken = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET) as any; // Verify the token using jsonwebtoken library
          
          if (decodedToken && decodedToken.userId) {
              return NextResponse.next();  // If a valid token is provided and verified, proceed to next middleware or route handler
          } else {
              return new Response('Unauthorized', { status: 401 }); // If the token does not contain userId in payload, respond with Unauthorized
          }
      } catch (error) {
          console.log(`JWT Error: ${error}`);
          return new Response('Unauthorized', { status: 401 }); // If there is an error while verifying the token, respond with Unauthorized
      }
    }
};
  
export default middleware;
